Onboarding flow

Merchant Requirements

Category

Requirement

Website

  • Active Website, Social Media and Apps are allowed

  • Marketplace is not allowed (i.e., Shopee, Lazada, etc)

Sub-merchant Name/Business Name

  • Merchant Name to be seen by customers in Online H5 payment page, and SMS. Value should be unique and max of 25 characters.

  • Business Name must match name in Website/URL

Restricted Businesses

  • Refer to table in Restricted Businesses/High Risk Industry for complete list of restricted businesses

    • Restricted = requires further documentation and approval

    • Rejected = cannot be onboarded

  • PF within a PF/ 2nd layer aggregator - NOT ALLOWED

Additional requirements for restricted businesses

  • Gcash EDD Requirements (see below)

  • Approval Form of GXI Mancom (℅ GCash AM)

High Risk Industry

*) notes: there were some exceptions made if sales are really pushing for this. MO will to coordinate with partnership for partnerships to negotiate with partner

The detail of new latest and updated restricted business in this document as of Oct 2024

Old List: July 2022 & July 2023

The following industries* are allowed under our current Aggregator set-up but are subjected for final clearance through our standard partner risk profiling measures. In some cases, additional documents** may also be required.

  1. Pseudo pharmaceuticals

  2. Government/Public Services and National Defense

  3. Lending/Financing

  4. Cooperative/Associations

  5. Telco

The following industries* requires an initial assessment from the management in addition to the standard risk profiling measures conducted. If approved, additional documentary requirements** would also be required.

  1. Merchants selling regulated products or services (with age restrictions)

  2. Merchants selling other dangerous materials

  3. Remittance Companies and FX Dealers and Money Changers

  4. Foundations/NGO/NPO/Charity

  5. Multi-Level Marketing

  6. Information Technology-Ads content advertising and crowdsourcing

  7. Company Service Providers

  8. Pawnshops

  9. Stocks Trading & Forex Trading

  10. Cryptocurrency

  11. Selling Adult Toys for recreational purposes

  12. Mining/Quarrying

  13. E-Wallets

  14. Designated Non-Financial Business and Profession (DNFBP) - jewelry dealers, dealers in precious metals, and dealers in precious stones, company service providers, lawyers and accountants and real estate developers and brokers.

*Identified Industries with secondary licenses must submit a copy. Please see Annex A for reference

**Also referred to as Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) documents. Please refer to Annex B

Prohibited Business/Industry

Specific to the industries below, no endorsement nor onboarding under our current set-up shall be allowed:

  1. Payment Facilitator

  2. Payment Solutions

  3. Entities engaged in Gambling/ Online Gaming and Online Casino/Casino

  4. Merchant selling substances designed to mimic illegal drugs

  5. Merchants selling drug paraphernalia

  6. Merchants selling Pornography and other obscene materials

  7. Merchants selling illegal products or services

  8. Merchants selling military/police supplies

  9. Merchants selling or offering services related to fake references or ID-providing services;

  10. Merchants selling goods in violation of Intellectual property or proprietary rights infringement-

  11. Merchants selling or engaged in business of goods in relation to sec.118 of Republic Act No. 10863 (Customs Modernization and Tariff Act);

  12. Entities offering false propaganda to get rich

  13. E-Sabong

Enhanced Due Diligence (EDD) Requirements

There are some industries that required an EDD, below are the requirements:

  1. Copy of Income Tax Return or latest Audited Financial Statement (if merchant is newly corporated, please submit notarized AFFIDAVIT of UNDERTAKING committing to submit the ITR/AFS once available and any of the following documents: Interim financial statements/Proof of property acquisition by the company/Proof of the company’s assets)

  2. Duly Accomplished Due Diligence Form signed by the Authorized Representative (if your business is non financial, please use this form. If your business is financial, please use this form).

  3. Duly Accomplished Know-Your-Customer (KYC) Form for each of the Primary Officers of the Company: President, Chief Financial Officer (CFO) or Treasurer, and Chief Operating Officer (COO) (form).

  4. 1 Valid ID for each of the Primary Officers of the Company: President, Chief Financial Officer (CFO) or Treasurer, and Chief Operating Officer (COO) and add 3 specimen signatures on every ID(s) submitted.

  5. For Non-Stock Corporations (Non-Government Organizations (NGO)/Non-Profit Organizations (NPO)/Foundation): Philippine Council for NGO Certification (PCNC) or Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Mandatory Disclosure Form.

Activation SLA

  • Channel Activation shown in merchant’s dashboard is 5 {{glossary.working days}}

  • Processing is 2-3 working days after MO send email submission to GCash

No

Activities

SLA

1

MO to receive request from CA dashboard and submit application to Gcash

1 working day

2

Gcash AM (Dianne) to review request and endorse to Gcash Ops for processing

1 working day

3

Gcash Ops team to process creation of sub-MID

2-3 working days

4

MO to activate Gcash via CA dashboard

1 working day

TOTAL

5-6 working days

5

If merchant is high risk, MO request for EDD requirements from merchant, and merchant need to accomplish them

+3 to 5 working days depending on merchant

6

Gcash Compliance to review EDD requirements

+1 to 2 working days

GCASH WEBPAY (Money-in)

Onboarding Step

Category

No

Steps

Owner

Merchant Applies for GCASH

1

  • Merchant click activate from merchant’s dashboard and Merchant fill information in customer dashboard

  • Once merchant activate their dashboard they will informed the SLA of the GCash activation

  • This request will goes to slack notification : channel activation GCASH

Merchant

MO Process merchant’s application

2

3

  • MO will check merchant’s submission from Channel Activation GCASH

  • MO will process GCASH submission in admin dashboard Channel Activation with status new

  • Click edit button to check all the detail in GCash Onboarding Info

MO

4

  • Check if all information is filled in and the merchant's website is active/ready. Make sure that the Parent and Child Industry matches the products on the merchant's website, and update if necessary.


NOTE:

  • If MO still needs more information from the merchant, MO will ask the merchant through sales and add notes in the merchant ops field and change the status from new submission to awaiting merchant data. Example as below:

MO

MO Processing to Excel sheet

5

MO will submit excel sheets with templates as required from GCash

This sheet generated from Admin dashboard

After all the new submission are processed, MO click the Download xlsx button

Select range of date we will collect from spreadsheet

And click download xlsx

Note: The range date for downloading the excel sheet has no limits

Admin dashboard will download details from the range date we’ve choose in previous step

Note :

Only merchants with status new submission will be downloaded. Do not change status from new submission to awaiting partner action manually.

Attached is the detail we get from GCash admin dashboard in excel file, and this spreadsheet is contains all merchant submission based on they’re registered for activation

Then, copy information from column C to column K and paste it to GCash OBEF template available in google drive.

*Note :

  • Column H (High Risk - Yes/No based on our risk industry)

  • Column I (High Risk - Risk Rating (Low, Med, High) based on our risk industry)

  • Column J (Age Restricted if any age restriction, example: vape 18+ then choose Yes)

  • Column Q for Enable GCredit, please always choose NO (request separately for merchants who want GCredit)

The title of the spreadsheet should be GCash OBEF_Xendit (date MM/DD/YY)_(Total Merchant Submitted) Batch #(follow the previous number). Example GCash OBEF_Xendit 032522_(6) Batch #94.

Download the spreadsheet in excel (.xlsx) format.

MO

MO Processing submit to GCash

6

Once spreadsheet is ready to be sent, prepare an email to GCash with details as follow :

Subject email : Online Submerchant Request Batch #1, [Date Submitted]

To : sales.ops@gcash.com, salesops@gcash.com

Cc: dianamarie.mapili@gcash.com, merchantops@xendit.ph

Hi Gcash Team,

Please see the attached batch of online sub-merchants today requested for Xendit merchants:

Batch # - [Date Submitted0

No of merchants: XX

Along with the attachment we downloaded from Google Drive (OBEF Sheet).

MO

GCash’s feedback

7

GCash team will response to our email within 2 or 3 days, they will give us credential code contains of :

  • Sub merchant name

  • Sub-MID

This is example of response from GCash by email :


And this is the detail of credential received from GCash

Partner

MO process for GCash activation

8

GCash Activation in Admin Dashboard

Once we get feedback from GCash, go to GCash channel activation dashboard

Filter merchant with status awaiting partner action

Click activate button, we’ll get a pop up window as below, insert Sub-Merchant ID provided by GCash and click confirm

The notification for activation will send to merchant automatically, as attached

MO

GGives/GCredit

  • GCredit is a pay later with credit line

  • GGives allows payment in installments

  • Onboarding Merchants to Gcash Money In also gives some merchants access to GGives and GCredit if based on the industry they submitted, they are not under restricted GCash Industry Sector Restrictions for GCredit/GGives

Onboarding Steps

Category

No

Steps

owner

Merchant Request for GGives/GCredit

1

Merchant request via CS/Sales/AM and raise to #mo-ph-request

Merchant

MO process for GGives/GCredit activation

2

MO to fill the form Xendit - Lending Products Activation Request Form

Download the sheet and shall only leave the requested merchant on the sheet

MO to send the request via email with subject GGives/GCredit Application Check | (merchant name) by informing below details :

  • Gcash sub-merchant MID

  • Website

  • Industry

And attach the sheet

MO

GCash process application

3

GCash will review the request every mid and end of the month

If GCash approves, MO should inform the requester and inform to try the feature on merchant’s end

*If GCash rejects, MO shall not take any action for re-appeal as the final decision sits on partner’s

Partner

GCASH LINK AND PAY (Tokenization)

  • Gcash Link and Pay is the tokenization product of Gcash for Money-In

  • The eligibility, restrictions and EDD requirements are the same as Money-in here

Requirements

  • Merchant needs to be onboarded with Gcash Webpay (Money-In) first before onboarding with Link and Pay

  • Submit the front end and back end flows (documentation) of each merchant.

  • Schedule a walkthrough meeting with the merchant and the GCash product team.

  • Share monthly projected volumes and transaction count.

  • Merchant Onboarding Form (MOF)

Onboarding Steps

No

Steps

Owner

1.

Collect the following requirements from the Merchant:

  1. Front end flow

  2. Back end flow

  3. Refund process flow

Sample:

Tonik Docs (Note: Do NOT share; for internal reference only)

  • Important: Show how linking and refunds will happen

Xendit – Sales AM / CPS / OIM

2.

  • Estimate the Merchant’s monthly projected volumes and transaction count.

Xendit – Sales AM / CPS / OIM

3.

Submit all requirements in the following L&P Tracker for PH Partnerships Initial Review

  • TPV, Txn Count, Sell Rate

  • FE, BE, Refund Flows

  • L&P Merchant Onboarding Form

    • For existing merchants: CPS to request from MO

    • For new merchants: MO to provide during onboarding process

Xendit – Sales AM / CPS / OIM

4.

Review all requirements and provide feedback on the submitted requirements to AM / CPS. (TAT: 3 days)

  • Mica Rose Nopuente will do an initial review of the requirements and inform sales if there are any changes needed before submission to GCash

Xendit – Partnerships

Mica Rose Nopuente

5.

Submit merchant requirements to GCash.

  • L&P Merchant Onboarding Form (via MO)

  • FE, BE, Refund Flows

Xendit – Partnerships

Mica Rose Nopuente

6.

GCash to confirm receipt of the requirements and provide feedback (TAT: 2 weeks from submission)

  • Mica Rose Nopuentewill inform the Sales AM / CPS of the status and feedback

GCash – Business Team

(c/o Mica Rose Nopuente)

7.

Once approved, schedule a walkthrough meeting with Xendit, GCash, and Merchant. Follow up calls may be needed depending on the outcome of the initial meeting.

  • Mica Rose Nopuente to lead on scheduling

Xendit Partnerships

Xendit Sales AM / CPS / OIM

GCash

Merchant

GCash to confirm if Merchant is approved for onboarding.

Post Onboarding Requests

Merchant request change Business Name

In GCash, nominated sub-merchant name in application will appear in Gcash payment page, and SMS, once merchant request to change business name, MO must make sure if website change or not then do following steps :

MO should fill the form, with below details :

  1. Requestor Full Name : Xendit Phillippines Inc.

  2. Requestor Email Address : merchantops@xendit.ph

  3. Business Name : (to fill the old business name)

  4. Date of Request : (the date when we request)

  5. General Change Request Details : (fill with below details)

Change Branch Name

Submerchant MID :

Current name :

Change to :

URL :

  1. Authorized Representative : Christian Reyes

  2. Signature : Request for signature on #mo-request

Once filling the form, MO should also send the updated OBEF with the new branch name as requested. MO will inform Gcash by email, and send merchant’s detail along with the attachments (form and OBEF)

Example Template email :

Subject email : GCash / Request To Change Submerchant Name - [Merchant Name]

To : dianamarie.mapili@gcash.com,salesops@gcash.com,sales.ops@gcash.com

cc :emeryne-j.jalijali@gcash.com,yummy.jalijali@gcash.com,merchantops@xendit.ph,Cherry Mijares

Hi Gcash Team,

Please kindly check if possible request to change this submerchant name :

Submerchant MID :

Submerchant Current name :

Change to :

URL website updated into :

Attached as well the General Change Request Form

Thank you!

After our request is processed by GCash team, MO will inform Digipay team in channel #p-ewallets-qrcodes-issues with following detail :

Merchant’s BID :

GSubmerchant Current Name:

Gcash Submerchant MID:

Submerchant New Name:

Ref : MO request to Digipay

Onboarding eHealthCare sub-merchants

As eHealthCare is already onboarded with us along with sub-merchants, GCash & Xendit have aligned on how to onboard. Below are the requirements to be submitted along with the OBEF sheet :

  1. PRC ID of the doctor

  2. Current year’s PTR (Professional Tax Receipt)

  3. Other supporting documents (doctor’s receipts) as needed - receipt given when doctor renews their PRC

  4. Personal details of doctor (please use the KYC Form)

  • Name

  • Birthdate

  • Birthplace

  • Address

  • Source of income

  • Contact information

  • Nationality

  • Nature of work

  • Specimen signature
